captures.alt: Kin Resolve synthetic Hartwell–Mercer dashboard showing active research cases and archive metrics.
captures.body: A clearly labeled fictional archive turns people, sources, cases, and next actions into one private research view.
captures.title: A private investigation dashboard

captures.alt: Kin Resolve synthetic GEDCOM source with a durable refresh queued for private review.
captures.body: A named GEDCOM source keeps its durable processing state; selecting a file never silently changes the archive.
captures.title: A durable GEDCOM source

captures.alt: Kin Resolve review screen showing incoming synthetic GEDCOM changes and policies for conflicts and deletions.
captures.body: Additions, edits, conflicts, and deletions are grouped under explicit review policies before apply.
captures.title: Review every change before apply

captures.alt: Kin Resolve fictional Mercer–March identity case showing competing hypotheses, confidence, and a reviewable research trail.
captures.body: The question, evidence, competing explanations, confidence, and next useful search stay together.
captures.title: Keep evidence and uncertainty together

captures.alt: Kin Resolve fictional Hartwell–Mercer source transcripts linked to private research context.
captures.body: Searchable transcripts remain close to the people and cases they inform; cohort one excludes binary source media.
captures.title: Keep source transcripts in context

captures.alt: Kin Resolve quality report prioritizing synthetic archive issues with no external AI used.
captures.body: Deterministic date, source, and privacy checks run inside the Kin Resolve deployment without an external AI provider.
captures.title: Check quality without an external AI provider

captures.alt: Kin Resolve developer API controls showing scoped expiring archive tokens without revealing a token secret.
captures.body: Create archive-bound, scoped, expiring tokens; secrets appear once and revocation denies the next request.
captures.title: Create scoped, revocable API access

captures.alt: Kin Resolve archive portability panel offering a full GEDCOM export and reviewable reversible imports.
captures.body: Download the archive as GEDCOM, preserve a review trail, and keep rollback and verified deletion paths visible.
captures.title: Keep an exit path

segments.text: Family trees preserve conclusions. Kin Resolve preserves questions, records, uncertainty, and next steps. This tour uses only fictional Hartwell–Mercer data.
Bring in a plain GEDCOM as a named, durable source. The original export is staged privately, processing state survives a refresh, and nothing enters the archive merely because a file was selected.
Incoming additions, edits, conflicts, and deletions become an explicit review. Safe changes can be approved together; ambiguity requires a decision; remote deletion keeps the local record by default.

Research stays organized around a question, not just a person. Evidence can strengthen or weaken competing hypotheses, confidence remains visible, and the case keeps the next search tied to the reason it matters.
Source transcripts remain close to the people and cases they inform. Cohort one deliberately excludes binary source media, so the hosted boundary stays narrow, understandable, and enforceable.
Quality and privacy checks run deterministically inside Kin Resolve. They prioritize date conflicts, source gaps, and sharing risks without sending the archive to an external AI provider.
The developer preview uses scoped, expiring, archive-bound tokens. A secret is shown once, only its prefix remains visible, and revocation denies the next request immediately.
The exit path stays visible: download a GEDCOM, keep the review trail, and request verified deletion. Apply without sending family records.
